# Accuracy Audit

corvus claims accuracy only where it has been measured on real silicon.
This document records, per function: the method, the measured error bounds,
and how they were measured. It is updated in the same change as any kernel
or test-gate change.

## Methodology

**Oracle.** Reference values are generated by mpmath at 50 decimal digits
and rounded to the nearest double (`tools/gen_*_reference.py`). Reference
files are checked in (`tests/data/`); each point-selection strategy is
documented in its generator and deliberately includes the worst cases for
the method under test (grid residual extremes, region boundaries at the
bit level, cancellation-prone ranges, subnormal-result bands).

**Metric.** ULP distance via the ordered-bits mapping (sign-magnitude to
monotonic integer), so adjacent doubles differ by 1 everywhere including
across ±0 and into the subnormals. "not-CR" = fraction of results that are
not the correctly rounded double.

**Gates.** `test_*_ulp` enforce the measured bounds with no margin; any
regression trips CI. Bounds are claimed only for tiers validated on native
hardware (lesson: an SSE2 subnormal bug in a prior project was invisible
under Rosetta emulation). Tier capping (`CORVUS_DISABLED_TARGETS`) counts
as native for any tier at or below the host CPU's best.

**Benchmarks** are not accuracy claims and live outside this document;
per-tier numbers in PLAN.md are labeled indicative unless taken on a quiet
machine.

## Validation matrix

| Function | AVX2 | SSE4 | SSSE3 | SSE2 | NEON | AVX-512 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| erf | ✅ 2026-07-20 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ 2026-07-21 | pending |
| erfc | ✅ 2026-07-21 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ 2026-07-21 | pending |

x86 validation: Kaby Lake i7-7820HQ, AppleClang (AVX2+FMA native; SSE
tiers via capping, which on this CPU also exercises the no-FMA code
paths); the same tier sweep also runs in CI on Linux/GCC. NEON validation:
Apple Silicon GitHub runner (native silicon, native FMA), Highway 1.4.0,
CI run 2026-07-21 — all ULP gates passed; per-run measured values appear
in the CI "ULP report" step from that date forward. AVX-512 requires the
Ryzen 7445 (hosted runners don't provide it).

## erf

**Bound: max 1 ULP over the full domain** (~98.5% correctly rounded on the
14,594-point reference).

Method: 1537-entry table on the grid r = j/256 over [0, 6], each entry
{E_hi, S, E_lo} with E_hi + E_lo a compensated erf(r) and S = erf'(r);
local Taylor correction in the exact residual d = a − r using Hermite-form
coefficients c2..c8 evaluated at run time. |x| ≥ 5.9215871957945071
saturates to ±1 (that bound is the smallest double whose erf rounds to 1,
so saturation is correctly rounded). The method was independently derived
(local Taylor expansion, Hermite-polynomial derivatives of exp(−x²)); the
generator self-checks the derivation against mpmath each run. Special
values: erf(±0) = ±0, erf(±inf) = ±1, NaN propagates with payload.

## erfc

**Bounds: max 1 ULP for |x| ≤ 6; max 1 ULP for subnormal results; max
5 ULP for normal results with x > 6** (measured identical on all four
validated tiers).

Method, core |x| ≤ 6: the erf table, assembled as compensated
1 ∓ erf(|x|) (Fast2Sum head plus exact residual) so the cancellation in
1 − erf never discards low bits; near erf saturation the table's E_lo term
is exactly −erfc(r) to full precision. The Taylor series runs to d⁸
because erfc requires the correction to *relative* erfc precision — the
d⁶ truncation term, harmless for erf in absolute terms, is up to ~2e-13
relative to erfc near x = 6.

Method, tail x > 6: erfc(a) = e^(−a²) · G(1/a) / a with
G(u) = a·e^(a²)·erfc(a) fitted per interval ([6,10], [10,17], [17,28];
degrees 11/10/8, Chebyshev-derived, coefficients selected per lane into a
single Horner pass). a² is split exactly into hi + lo before the
exponential (a ½-ULP error in a² alone would cost ~a²/2 ULP after
exponentiation — ~360 ULP at a = 27); the split uses FMA where native and
Dekker's algorithm on non-FMA targets, where an emulated fused op silently
returns a zero residual. The 5-ULP tail bound is entirely the backend
vector exp's contribution — every other stage of the path measures at the
1-ULP level; a compensated in-house exp is the known path to tightening
it. Results underflow gradually past x ≈ 26.5; erfc(x) = 0 for
x ≥ ~27.3. Special values: erfc(±0) = 1, erfc(−inf) = 2, erfc(+inf) = 0,
NaN propagates with payload.

Negative x mirrors through the compensated core (not 2 − erfc(|x|), which
would reround) up to |x| = 6; beyond, 2 − tail rounds to exactly 2, which
is the correctly rounded result everywhere it applies.
